015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025/**
026 * The HTTP protocol version that appears in request and response start lines (e.g. {@code "HTTP/1.1"}).
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Mirrors the semantics of {@code org.apache.http.ProtocolVersion} without the Apache HttpCore dependency.
030 * The protocol name and major/minor numbers are stored separately so callers can compare versions numerically.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Create instances via {@link #of(String, int, int)} or {@link #parse(String)}:
034 * <p class='bjava'>
035 *    <jc>// "HTTP/1.1"</jc>
036 *    HttpProtocolVersion <jv>v1</jv> = HttpProtocolVersion.<jsf>HTTP_1_1</jsf>;
037 *
038 *    <jc>// Parse from a wire string</jc>
039 *    HttpProtocolVersion <jv>v2</jv> = HttpProtocolVersion.<jsm>parse</jsm>(<js>"HTTP/2.0"</js>);
040 * </p>
041 *
042 * @param protocol The protocol name (e.g. {@code "HTTP"}). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
043 * @param major The major version number (e.g. {@code 1}).
044 * @param minor The minor version number (e.g. {@code 1}).
045 *
046 * @since 10.0.0
047 */
048public record HttpProtocolVersion(String protocol, int major, int minor) {
049
050   /** {@code HTTP/1.0}. */
051   public static final HttpProtocolVersion HTTP_1_0 = new HttpProtocolVersion("HTTP", 1, 0);
052
053   /** {@code HTTP/1.1}. */
054   public static final HttpProtocolVersion HTTP_1_1 = new HttpProtocolVersion("HTTP", 1, 1);
055
056   /** {@code HTTP/2.0}. */
057   public static final HttpProtocolVersion HTTP_2_0 = new HttpProtocolVersion("HTTP", 2, 0);
058
059   /**
060    * Canonical constructor.
061    *
062    * @param protocol The protocol name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
063    * @param major The major version (must be {@code >= 0}).
064    * @param minor The minor version (must be {@code >= 0}).
065    */
066   public HttpProtocolVersion {
067      assertArgNotNull("protocol", protocol);
068      if (major < 0)
069         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Major version must be >= 0, got " + major);
070      if (minor < 0)
071         throw new IllegalArgumentException("Minor version must be >= 0, got " + minor);
072   }
073
074   /**
075    * Creates a protocol version with the given protocol name and version numbers.
076    *
077    * @param protocol The protocol name (e.g. {@code "HTTP"}). Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
078    * @param major The major version number.
079    * @param minor The minor version number.
080    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public static HttpProtocolVersion of(String protocol, int major, int minor) {
083      return new HttpProtocolVersion(protocol, major, minor);
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * Parses a wire-format protocol version string.
088    *
089    * <p>
090    * Accepts the standard {@code PROTOCOL/major.minor} form (e.g. {@code "HTTP/1.1"}) and the abbreviated
091    * {@code PROTOCOL/major} form (e.g. {@code "HTTP/2"}, which is parsed as major=2, minor=0).
092    *
093    * @param s The protocol version string. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
094    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
095    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the string cannot be parsed.
096    */
097   public static HttpProtocolVersion parse(String s) {
098      assertArgNotNull("s", s);
099      var slash = s.indexOf('/');
100      if (slash < 0)
101         throw iaex("Invalid protocol version: {0}", s);
102      var protocol = s.substring(0, slash);
103      var rest = s.substring(slash + 1);
104      var dot = rest.indexOf('.');
105      Supplier<RuntimeException> err = () -> iaex("Invalid protocol version: {0}", s);
106      if (dot < 0)
107         return new HttpProtocolVersion(protocol, parseInt(rest, err), 0);
108      return new HttpProtocolVersion(protocol, parseInt(rest.substring(0, dot), err), parseInt(rest.substring(dot + 1), err));
109   }
110
111   /**
112    * Returns the canonical wire-format string (e.g. {@code "HTTP/1.1"}).
113    *
114    * @return The wire-format string. Never <jk>null</jk>.
115    */
116   @Override /* Object */
117   public String toString() {
118      return protocol + "/" + major + "." + minor;
119   }
120}
